name: Carmen Teresa Navas
photo_caption: PORTRAIT — CARMEN TERESA NAVAS · PHOTO PENDING FAMILY APPROVAL
epithet: Mother of Víctor Hugo Quero Navas. Still waiting to be told the truth.
summary: Carmen Teresa Navas is 82 years old. Her son Víctor Hugo Quero Navas was detained on January 1, 2025 and transferred to Tocorón Prison. The family reports that since then he has been forcibly disappeared — they have never been given official information about his whereabouts. According to information that has reached them, Víctor Hugo died on July 27, 2025. His death has not been officially confirmed. What remains for Carmen is the absence of truth.

story.title: What we know, and what we do not
paragraphs: Carmen Teresa Navas is the mother of Víctor Hugo Quero Navas. On January 1, 2025, her son was detained and transferred to Tocorón Prison. From the moment of that transfer, his family has not been given official information about where he is, how he is, or whether he is alive.

On July 27, 2025, Carmen received word — through unofficial channels — that Víctor Hugo had died. That word has not been confirmed by any authority. No body has been delivered. No certificate has been issued. No explanation has been given. For the family, this is not the end of a story. It is the absence of one.

subsections.title: The right to know
paragraphs: Under international human rights law, families of the disappeared have the right to know the truth: where their loved one is held, what condition they are in, and — if they are no longer alive — when, where, and how they died, and where their remains rest. Carmen Teresa Navas has not been given any of this.

This profile exists because she has asked for it to exist. It will be updated the moment any official information is delivered. Until then, the record will stand as it is — incomplete, because so is the truth.

events.title: Víctor Hugo Quero Navas is detained
events.description: Víctor Hugo, son of Carmen Teresa Navas, is detained and shortly afterward transferred to Tocorón Prison.

events.title: No official information
events.description: Since the transfer, the family has not been given any official information about Víctor Hugo's whereabouts, condition, or status. They consider him forcibly disappeared.

events.title: Reported death — unconfirmed
events.description: The family receives word, through unofficial channels, that Víctor Hugo has died. As of this profile's last update, no authority has confirmed his death. No body or remains have been delivered. No certificate has been issued.
events.source: Source: family report · unconfirmed by any official source

events.title: Carmen is still waiting to be told the truth
events.description: Carmen Teresa Navas, 82, continues to ask the only question a mother can ask: where is my son.
events.source: Last update on this profile: pending family review

voices.title: Voices — pending
voices.description: Direct testimonies from Carmen and from those who know the family will appear here when collected and consented to. We do not publish voices without explicit, recorded consent. This space is intentionally left open.

testimonies.text: Awaiting recorded testimony from Carmen Teresa Navas.
testimonies.name: Carmen Teresa Navas

testimonies.text: Awaiting testimony from a member of Carmen's family.

support.label: How to stand with Carmen
support.title: Three things you can do, today.
support.description: No family in this archive asks for your sympathy. They ask only that the truth be told, and that their loved ones not be forgotten. Here are three concrete things you can do — none of them take more than ten minutes.

actions.label: Ask
actions.title: Ask the question Carmen is asking
actions.description: Send a written request to the relevant authorities asking for the whereabouts and status of Víctor Hugo Quero Navas. We provide the addresses and a template letter in two languages.

actions.label: Carry the name
actions.title: Share Carmen and Víctor Hugo's profile
actions.description: A short shareable card in two languages. The smallest form of accompaniment a family of the disappeared can receive is the act of someone, somewhere, knowing their name.

actions.label: Sustain
actions.title: Sustain the work that holds the record
actions.description: This archive is built by hand by a small staff. A monthly gift sustains the work — verification, family interviews, legal review, and translation in both languages.
